methods.description: This method involves using phylogenetic networks to analyze evolutionary processes that cannot be represented by a single phylogenetic tree. The Neighbor-Net method in SplitsTree is used to perform split decomposition analysis of haplotypes.

steps.description: Use the Neighbor-Net method implemented in SplitsTree version 4.13.1 to perform split decomposition analysis of haplotypes.
steps.searchable_summary: perform split decomposition analysis using Neighbor-Net in SplitsTree

steps.description: Infer the phylogeny of the 20 sickle haplotypes, rooted by the ancestral haplotype.
Analyze clusters derived from the ancestral haplotype and their associated mutations.

methods.description: This method involves retrieving and processing whole-genome-sequence data from various genomic projects to establish phased haplotypes and assess linkage disequilibrium.

steps.description: Retrieve whole-genome-sequence data from the 1000 Genomes Project, African Genome Variation Project, and Qatar.
steps.searchable_summary: retrieve genome sequence data from multiple projects

steps.description: Use SHAPEIT2 to estimate a scaffold of long-range phased haplotypes given array-based genotype data and family information.
Use a combination of Beagle and SHAPEIT2 to jointly analyze the long-range phased haplotypes and di-allelic variants.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze phased haplotypes and variants using Beagle and SHAPEIT2

steps.description: Use MVNcall to place multi-allelic and structural variants onto the haplotype scaffold.
steps.searchable_summary: place variants onto haplotype scaffold using MVNcall

steps.description: Assess linkage disequilibrium in the 1000 Genomes Project data using the --hap-r2 function for phased haplotypes.
